title: Pulitzer Prize Winner Dan Yergin on How the Iran War Is Reshaping the Energy Map, and the Race to Power AI
gist: Promotional teaser for a Bridgewater podcast with energy expert Daniel Yergin (S&P Global vice chairman) discussing how the Iran war, AI power demand, and modern mercantilism all converge on energy markets. The actual analytical content is gated behind the podcast; only the framing survives in this text.

themes.theme: Energy at the center of macro dynamics

insights.claim: Bridgewater frames energy markets as the common node linking today's three biggest macro forces: modern mercantilism, the rise of AI, and the war in Iran.
insights.speaker: Bridgewater Research
insights.so_what: Positions energy as the cross-asset linchpin for trading geopolitics, AI build-out, and trade policy simultaneously rather than as siloed themes.
insights.quote: it’s striking how often the energy market sits at the center of them
insights.locator: When we look at the major dynamics reshaping the world today

insights.claim: Daniel Yergin characterizes the energy disruption from the Iran war as 'the mother of all supply shocks,' raising the question of how producers respond and how a global push for energy security reshapes markets and economies.
insights.speaker: Bridgewater Research
insights.so_what: Signals a structurally bullish/volatile oil-supply backdrop and a policy shift toward energy security that could lift commodity and defensive-energy exposure.
insights.quote: the mother of all supply shocks
insights.locator: Across the conversation, Dan, Atul, and Ian explore

insights.claim: The podcast frames the AI build-out as a 'race to power' it, flagging both the demand surge for energy and a brewing political debate over AI's energy use.
insights.speaker: Bridgewater Research
insights.so_what: Reinforces the AI-power demand thesis (utilities, generation, grid) while flagging political/regulatory risk to AI energy consumption as a watch item.
insights.quote: how the race to power the AI build-out is playing out, the political debate around AI energy use
insights.locator: how the race to power the AI build-out is playing out
